title: lantana camara f. portoricensis
intro: Lantana camara f. portoricensis, also known as the Puerto Rican buttonquail weed or lantana shrub, is a flowering plant native to the Caribbean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of lantana camara f. portoricensis include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to consult a medical professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
